volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 4.5.680 - Section 4.9.741b)

sections.label: Section 4.5.680
sections.original: At ne cum argento protinam permittas domum ,
sections.translation: But don ’ t you at once be changing your quarters with the money , I recommend you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.681b
sections.original: Quod te dignumst , me dignum esse vis ?
sections.translation: What ’ s worthy of yourself , do you take to be worthy of me ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.682
sections.original: Tace , párce voci : praeda progreditur foras .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue ; lower your voice ; the spoil is coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.6.683
sections.original: Probae hic argenti sunt sexaginta minae ,
sections.translation: Sixty minae of assayed silver are here ( pointing at the bag .) less two didrachms .

sections.label: Section 4.6.684b
sections.translation: What ’ s the meaning of those didrachms ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.685
sections.original: Cruminam hanc emere aut facere uti remigret domum .
sections.translation: To pay for this bag , or else to cause it to come home again .

sections.label: Section 4.6.686
sections.original: Ne non sat esses leno , id metuebas miser ,
sections.translation: Lest you mightn ’ t be enough of a Procurer , did you fear , wretched , filthy , avaricious creature , that you might lose your bag ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.688
sections.original: Sine quaeso . quando lenost , nil mirum facit .
sections.translation: Pray , let him alone ; since he is a Procurer , he isn ’ t doing anything surprising .

sections.label: Section 4.6.689
sections.original: Lucro faciundo ego auspicavi in hunc diem :
sections.translation: I ’ ve judged from omens that I should make some profit to-day ;

sections.label: Section 4.6.690
sections.original: nil mihi tam parvist , quin me id pigeat perdere .
sections.translation: nothing is of value so small to me , but that I grudge to lose it . Come , take this , will you ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.691b
sections.translation: Place it around my neck , if it is not too much trouble .

sections.label: Section 4.6.692b
sections.translation: Certainly , it shall be done .

sections.label: Section 4.6.692c
sections.translation: Is there anything else that you wish with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.693b
sections.translation: Why are you in such haste ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.693c
sections.translation: My business is of that nature ; the letters that have been entrusted me , I want to deliver ;

sections.label: Section 4.6.695
sections.original: geminum autem fratrem servire audivi hic meum ,
sections.translation: and I ’ ve heard that my twin-brother ’ s a slave here ; I wish to be off to seek him out , and redeem him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.697
sections.original: Atque edepol tu me commonuisti haú male .
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , you ’ ve not badly put me in mind of it ; I think that I ’ ve seen here one very like you in figure , of just the same size .

sections.label: Section 4.6.699b
sections.translation: Why , it must surely be my brother [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.6.700
sections.translation: But we ’ d like to know what your name is .

sections.label: Section 4.6.701
sections.translation: What does it matter to us to know ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.701b
sections.translation: Listen then , that you may know ; my name is Lying- speakerus [*] , Virgin-seller-onides , Trifle-great-talker-ides , Silver-screwer-outides , Thee-worthy-to-talk-to-ides , Wheedler-out-of-coin-ides ,

sections.label: Section 4.6.705
sections.translation: What-he-has-once-got-hold-of-ides , Never-again-part-with-it-ides .

sections.label: Section 4.6.706
sections.original: Eu hercle , nomen multimodis scriptumst tuom .
sections.translation: Dear me ; upon my faith , this name of yours is written in many ways .

sections.label: Section 4.6.707
sections.original: Ita sunt Persarum mores , longa nomina ,
sections.translation: Such is the way with the Persians ; we have long names of many words twisted together . Do you wish for anything else ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.709b
sections.translation: Farewell !

sections.label: Section 4.6.709c
sections.original: Et vos , nam animus iam in navist meus .
sections.translation: And you farewell ; for my mind ’ s aboard ship already .

sections.label: Section 4.6.710
sections.original: Cras ires potius , hodie hic cenares .
sections.translation: You ’ d better have gone to-morrow , and dined here to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.6.710b
sections.translation: ( SAGARISTIO is going .) Farewell !

sections.label: Section 4.7.711
sections.original: Postquam illic hinc abiit , dicere hic quidvis licet .
sections.translation: Since that fellow ’ s gone , I may say here whatever I please . This day has assuredly shone a gainful one for you ; for you ’ ve not been buying her , but making a clear profit of her .

sections.label: Section 4.7.714
sections.original: Ille quidem iam scit , quid negoti gesserit ,
sections.translation: He indeed quite understands what he has been about ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.715
sections.original: qui mihi furtivam meo periclo vendidit ,
sections.translation: in having sold me a stolen woman at my own risk ; he has got the money , and taken himself off . How do I know now whether she mayn ’ t be claimed at once ? Whither am I to follow him ? To the Persians , nonsense .

sections.label: Section 4.7.718b
sections.translation: I imagined that my services would be a cause for thankfulness with you .

sections.label: Section 4.7.719b
sections.translation: Why , yes , indeed , I do return you thanks ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.720
sections.original: tibi , Toxile , habeo ; nam te sensi sedulo
sections.translation: Toxilus , for I found that you zealously gave me your assistance .

sections.label: Section 4.7.721b
sections.translation: What , I , to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.722
sections.original: Attat , oblitus sum intus dudum edicere
sections.translation: In seriousness , yes . [*] By-the-bye , I forgot just now to give some directions in-doors , which I intended to be given . Do keep watch on her .

sections.label: Section 4.7.723b
sections.translation: She ’ s all safe , for certain .

sections.label: Section 4.7.724
sections.translation: My father ’ s delaying now .

sections.label: Section 4.7.724b
sections.translation: What , if I put him in mind ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.724c
sections.translation: It ’ s full time .

sections.label: Section 4.7.725
sections.original: Heus , Saturio , exi . nunc est illa occasio
sections.translation: Hallo ! Saturio , come forward ; now ’ s the opportunity for taking vengeance on the enemy .

sections.label: Section 4.7.726b
sections.translation: See , here I am . Have I delayed at all ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.727
sections.original: Age , illúc abscede procul e conspectu , tace ;
sections.translation: Well , go you off there at a distance out of sight ; keep silence . When you see me talking to the Procurer , do you then make a row .

sections.label: Section 4.7.729b
sections.translation: A word ’ s enough to the wise .

sections.label: Section 4.7.730
sections.translation: You , when I ’ m gone —

sections.label: Section 4.7.730b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you hold your tongue ? I know what it is you want .

sections.label: Section 4.8.731
sections.translation: On coming into the house , I lashed them all with the whip ; my house and furniture are in such a dirty state .

sections.label: Section 4.8.733
sections.translation: Are you returned at last ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.733b
sections.translation: I ’ m returned .

sections.label: Section 4.8.733c
sections.translation: Assuredly , I have this day done you many services .

sections.label: Section 4.8.734b
sections.translation: I confess it ; I give you thanks .

sections.label: Section 4.8.735
sections.translation: Do you want anything else with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.735b
sections.translation: That happiness may . attend you .

sections.label: Section 4.8.736
sections.original: Pol istúc quidem omen iám ego usurpabo domi ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , all that indeed I shall surely enjoy at home now ; for I shall now go take my place at table with your freed-woman .

sections.label: Section 4.9.738
sections.original: Nisi ego illum hominem perdo , perii . atque optume
sections.translation: If I don ’ t prove the destruction of that fellow —

sections.label: Section 4.9.739b
sections.translation: Most welcome , my dear father .

sections.label: Section 4.9.740
sections.translation: Welcome , my child .

sections.label: Section 4.9.740b
sections.translation: That Persian has utterly ruined me !

sections.label: Section 4.9.741
sections.translation: This is my father .

sections.label: Section 4.9.741b
sections.translation: Ha !— what ?— father ? I ’ m utterly undone ! Why then , in my misery , do I delay to bewail my sixty minae ?
